definition: The standing stability of the lawful pattern of things.
notes: The repository keeps stability of the pattern as the default because it stays readable while preserving the lawful, non-essentialist force of the term. Stability of dhamma remains acceptable in source-facing explanation where keeping dhamma audible matters. The Buddhadasa usage profile and the Idappaccayatā practical talk profile support the broader natural-law cluster here by reinforcing that the pattern is stable as lawfulness, not as a hidden substance behind conditions.

context_rules.context: In source-facing explanation where dhamma should stay audible
context_rules.notes: Use when preserving the family tie matters more than smoothing the English.

authority_basis.scope: Supports lawful-pattern handling over scholastic abstraction.
Supports treating dhammatthiti as the stable standing of conditional law rather than as essence-language.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'stability of the pattern' as the default rendering unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not import fixed-essence language or later scholastic system-talk where the early-text function is simply lawful standing order.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift into essence-language or over-systematized scholastic paraphrase.
